summary: chalk-pack postinstall exfiltrates host and filesystem information
details: The package's postinstall.js executes on `npm install` and combines filesystem enumeration (fs.existsSync checks against paths derived from os.homedir()) with host identity collection (os.hostname(), os.userInfo()) and outbound HTTP transmission (require('http') used to send the gathered data). The package name is also a likely typosquat of the widely-installed `chalk` package, which is consistent with a confusion-attack distribution strategy. The combination of install-time host/user fingerprinting plus filesystem probing plus HTTP egress is the structural shape of a credential/host-reconnaissance beacon, with no legitimate purpose for a package presenting itself as a chalk-related utility.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
